title: SysY to Koopa IR / RISC-V Compiler (Rust)
summary: A compiler for the SysY language (C subset) that translates source code first into Koopa IR (frontend) and then into RISC-V assembly (backend). The frontend consists of an expression builder, a statement builder, and a declaration builder feeding a top-level compilation-unit entry point; the backend pairs an LRU register allocator with an instruction-level code generator. AST definitions and the LALRPOP parser are pre-existing and require no implementation.
instructionPreview: This project is an undergraduate compiler for the SysY language (a C subset) that translates source code into Koopa IR and RISC-V assembly. The compiler is written in Rust and uses the `lalrpop` parser generator along with the `koopa` IR library. It supports expressions (arithmetic, logical with short-circuit evaluation), control flow (if/else, while, break, continue), functions (definitions, calls, parameters), variable/constant declarations, and arrays (including multi-dimensional).
instruction:
This project is an undergraduate compiler for the SysY language (a C subset) that translates source code into Koopa IR and RISC-V assembly. The compiler is written in Rust and uses the `lalrpop` parser generator along with the `koopa` IR library. It supports expressions (arithmetic, logical with short-circuit evaluation), control flow (if/else, while, break, continue), functions (definitions, calls, parameters), variable/constant declarations, and arrays (including multi-dimensional).

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present. The compiler binary is built with `cargo build --release` and called as:
  `/workspace/target/release/compiler-lab -koopa <input.c> -o <output.koopa>`
  `/workspace/target/release/compiler-lab -riscv <input.c> -o <output.asm>`

The compiler has two main subsystems that need to be implemented: the IR builder (frontend) and the assembly builder (backend). Functions marked with `todo!()` need complete implementations.

## Modules to Implement

- **IR Builder — Compilation Unit** (`src/ir_builder/mod.rs`): The top-level IR generation entry point. Responsible for declaring SysY library functions (getint, getch, getarray, putint, putch, putarray, starttime, stoptime) and building all compilation units. (~1 function to implement)

- **IR Builder — Declarations** (`src/ir_builder/build_declarations.rs`): Translates function definitions, variable declarations (local and global), and constant declarations into Koopa IR. Handles parameter allocation, symbol table management, array type construction, aggregate initializer flattening, and storing aggregate values element-by-element into local allocations. (~4 functions to implement)

- **IR Builder — Expressions** (`src/ir_builder/build_expressions.rs`): Translates SysY expressions into Koopa IR values. Covers binary operations with constant folding, logical OR/AND with short-circuit evaluation using basic blocks, unary expressions (plus, minus, not), function calls with parameter type checking, primary expressions (lvalues, numbers, parenthesized), and multi-dimensional array element access via getelemptr/getptr. (~5 functions to implement)

- **IR Builder — Statements** (`src/ir_builder/build_statements.rs`): Translates all SysY statement types into Koopa IR: assignments, expression statements, blocks, if/else with branch/jump wiring, while loops with break/continue target block stacks, break, continue, and return statements. (~1 large function to implement)

- **Assembly Builder — Code Generation** (`src/assembly_builder/build_assembly.rs`): The backend that converts Koopa IR programs into RISC-V assembly. Generates `.data`/`.text` sections for global variables and functions. Translates each IR instruction kind (return, binary, alloc, store, load, jump, branch, call, getptr, getelemptr) into the corresponding RISC-V instructions. Maps IR binary operations to RISC-V opcodes. Handles global aggregate initialization. (~4 functions to implement)

- **Assembly Builder — Register Management** (`src/assembly_builder/mod.rs`): Implements the register allocator and value table that manages how IR values are mapped to RISC-V registers using an LRU eviction strategy. Provides stack frame offset calculations for store/load/add operations (handling both short and long immediate offsets), register spilling to memory, LRU-based temporary register allocation, value-to-register loading (integers, globals, locals), value assignment between registers, absolute address computation for array elements, and saving/restoring global and local variables at basic block boundaries. (~9 functions to implement)

After implementing all functions, rebuild with `cargo build --release` before running tests.

moduleDag.project: SysY to Koopa IR / RISC-V Compiler (Rust)
moduleDag.description: A compiler for the SysY language (C subset) that translates source code first into Koopa IR (frontend) and then into RISC-V assembly (backend). The frontend consists of an expression builder, a statement builder, and a declaration builder feeding a top-level compilation-unit entry point; the backend pairs an LRU register allocator with an instruction-level code generator. AST definitions and the LALRPOP parser are pre-existing and require no implementation.

nodes.label: IR Builder — Expressions
nodes.description: Translates SysY expressions into Koopa IR values. Covers binary operations with compile-time constant folding, logical OR/AND with short-circuit evaluation via BasicBlocks, unary expressions (plus, minus, not), function calls with parameter count and type checking, primary expressions (lvalues, numbers, parenthesized subexpressions), and multi-dimensional array element access via getelemptr/getptr.

nodes.label: Assembly Builder — Register Allocator
nodes.description: Implements the LRU-based register allocator and value table (MyBBValueTable). Provides stack-frame store/load/add helpers handling both short and long immediates, LRU-eviction register selection (get_tmp_reg), register spilling (save_register), value-to-register loading for integers/globals/locals (want_to_visit_value), value-to-value copy (assign_v1_to_v2), absolute address computation for array elements (get_absolute_location), and bulk save routines for global and local variables at basic-block boundaries.

nodes.label: IR Builder — Statements
nodes.description: Translates all SysY statement types into Koopa IR: assignment (lvalue store), expression statements, block scoping with new symbol-table frames, if/else with branch/jump wiring across BasicBlocks, while loops with break/continue target-block stacks, break and continue unconditional jumps, and return with optional value.

nodes.label: Assembly Builder — RISC-V Code Generation
nodes.description: Converts a complete Koopa IR program into RISC-V assembly. Emits .data/.text sections for global variables (integer, zero-init, aggregate) and function definitions. Translates every IR instruction kind (return, binary, alloc, store, load, jump, branch, call, getptr, getelemptr) into RISC-V instruction sequences, manages function prologue/epilogue with stack-frame sizing, handles caller-saved registers around calls, and emits recursive .word directives for global aggregates.

nodes.label: IR Builder — Declarations
nodes.description: Translates function definitions (parameter allocation, symbol table wiring, body Block dispatch), variable declarations (local and global, with optional initializers), and constant declarations into Koopa IR. Implements recursive aggregate initializer construction (build_new_aggregate) and flattening of aggregate initializers into per-element store instructions (aggregate_to_store_insts).

nodes.label: IR Builder — Compilation Unit Entry Point
nodes.description: Top-level IR generation entry point (CompUnit::build). Declares all eight SysY standard-library functions (getint, getch, getarray, putint, putch, putarray, starttime, stoptime) as Koopa IR function declarations, then iterates over every top-level Unit in the CompUnit and dispatches to function-definition or declaration builders.

edges.label: statements evaluate conditions, RHS values, and return expressions by calling exp.build
declarations directly call exp.build for initializer value evaluation and array shape computation
FuncDef::build calls block.build, which requires BasicStmt::build from the statements builder
CompUnit::build iterates over Units and calls FuncDef::build and Decl::build from the declarations builder
every IR instruction translation in build_assembly.rs calls allocator methods (want_to_visit_value, save_register, assign_v1_to_v2, get_absolute_location, etc.)
